title: Bucket Types, Endpoints, Regions, and S3 Express · Execution Trace: Region Location Routing
summary: A client hard-codes path-style URLs, assumes global ordering and lifecycle features, and deploys latency-sensitive work without selecting a compatible bucket type or zone. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the region location routing increment in bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Construct regional virtual-hosted and zonal endpoints without confusing region, availability zone, bucket name, TLS name, or path style.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Region Location Routing · Theory Region Location Routing
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5764 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Region Location Routing contract for the supplied data.
